goal: Predict bulk_modulus, density, tg for PHYC (*CC*)
assumptions: polymer_rules.json defaults are validated for this class (confidence=high), EXCEPT the equilibration stage, which is overridden by the revision.md corrected protocol (Priority 0, R1 Major #4) to fix a prior ~25% density over-estimation (1.19 vs exp 0.95 g/cm3).

This is replicate 2 of 5 (seeds 1004/1005/1006). Seeds are independent per-replicate (not locked to a single REVISION_PARAMS.md baseline — PE has no registry row; seeds continued from replicate 1 triple 1001/1002/1003). Seeds are recorded for the orchestrator (cross-track rule 2); gen_prompt.py does not consume them.

uncertainties.name: kinetic_over_densification_cooling_rate
uncertainties.note: Prior PE run over-densified to ~1.19 g/cm3 (exp ~0.95). The revision.md fix asks for a >=2x slower melt->300 K cool, but the cool ramp N_STEPS=steps_npt is atom-count-tiered and is NOT expressible via decided_params or any generate_equilibration_workflow argument (no cool_steps override; t_equil_ns is display-only in gen_prompt, never threaded). add_melt_npt=true provides a PARTIAL ~1.28x slowdown on the glass-formation descent (cool segment shortens from 620->300 K=320 K to 550->300 K=250 K over the same steps_npt). The residual over-densification risk is therefore the dominant uncertainty; fast_density_screen (small-cell short NPT) cheaply reveals whether density still overshoots ~0.95 before committing the full cell. Full closure requires a code change (cool-ramp step override) or a manual intermediate cool stage — out of planner scope.

seeds.note: replicate 2 of 5; independent per-replicate seeds (continued from replicate 1 triple 1001/1002/1003); recorded for orchestrator (cross-track rule 2), not consumed by gen_prompt.py

evidence.claim: Ramos 2015: TraPPE-UA PE Tg=187K vs exp 185-195K, density ±7%. Branched types (PP, PIB): UA types exist but no polymer-Tg validation — use with medium confidence.

TraPPE-UA is a non-electrostatic UA force field; no explicit partial charges are placed on sites. Gasteiger is a nominal label for the charge_method field but carries zero weight: the EMC builder assigns no charges to a pure CH2/CH3 backbone. Charge assignment cost is effectively zero (no QM step needed).

PE backbone is pure C/H (no heteroatoms); TraPPE-UA |q|=0 on all sites. electrostatics_decision_guide: pure C/H backbones with |q|<0.1e MAY use lj_cut; heteroatom/|q|>0.1e MUST use pppm. lj_cut eliminates kspace overhead (relevant for UA cells where kspace would be ~80% wasted compute). No long-range Coulomb contribution to density or Tg for apolar polyethylene.
DP=120 exceeds Fox-Flory plateau (dp_min=60) for flexible PE backbone; nchain=20 (>10) satisfies revision.md 'larger system (>10 chains)' requirement for the density re-run.

decisions.choice: bilinear fit R^2>=0.80; T sweep 100-450 K brackets exp Tg~195 K with >100 K margin on both sides; tg_t_step=20 K (>=10 bins); cooling-rate artifact mitigated by three-rate multirate sweep (40/160/640 K/ns) and extrapolation to low rate.

evidence.claim: PE experimental Tg ~185-205 K (Boyer 1973; Ramos 2015). Sweep 100-450 K gives 95 K margin below and 245 K above experimental Tg, bracketing it comfortably. At 20 K/step this yields 17 temperature bins across the 100-450 K window (>4 minimum). Three cooling rates (40/160/640 K/ns) mitigate systematic rate artifact via linear extrapolation to zero rate.

decisions.choice: bulk_modulus via Murnaghan EOS NPT pressure sweep (primary) at T_workflow_K=300 K; PE is RUBBERY at 300 K (exp Tg~195 K < 300 K), so routing is Murnaghan-rubbery; volume-fluctuation cross-check only; 3-direction uniaxial deform fallback if fit_converged=False or B0_prime outside [4,20].

evidence.claim: Wu (2019) Comput. Mater. Sci. validates Murnaghan NPT compression for polymer K: faster and more reliable than volume-fluctuation for low-modulus rubbery polymers. Symmetric ±1000 atm pressure sweep around 1 atm gives 5 volume-pressure points; Murnaghan EOS fit yields K_0 (isothermal bulk modulus at 300 K). For rubbery PE at 300 K the expected K is ~1-3 GPa (compliant, high volume response per atm — favorable for Murnaghan signal-to-noise).

Born+NVT REMOVED (PCFF+PPPM virial incompatibility — K_Born 8-15x too high, Var(P) ~10^7x too high, failed 3/3 pipeline runs PMMA4/PVC1/PEEK1 2026-06-21). Not applicable here (TraPPE-UA not PCFF), but Born is retired pipeline-wide.

PE (PHYC, TraPPE-UA) is rubbery at T_workflow_K=300 K: exp Tg ~195 K < 300 K. is_glassy=False → Murnaghan rubbery path (npt equilibrated at 300 K as baseline). Glassy path (npt_prod300_out.data at 300 K with ±1000 atm) would apply if Tg>300 K — not the case here.

alternatives.known_error: Requires very long NVT runs for low-modulus rubbery polymers; noisy for K<5 GPa; cross-check only per pipeline policy.
Rate-dependent at accessible MD strain rates; anisotropy check needed (isotropy_delta_pct<20%); used as fallback only if Murnaghan fit fails.

decisions.choice: Corrected equilibration protocol: t_equil_ns=10 (2x standard), npt_prod_ns=10 (2x standard, 300 K density extraction), add_melt_npt=true with melt hold ~2 ns at 550 K; density-target detection gate added at equil-check.

evidence.claim: Prior PE run over-densified to ~1.19 g/cm3 vs experimental ~0.95 (+25%). revision.md Priority 0 (R1 Major #4) prescribes >=2x extended NPT equilibration, >10 chains, and a lower cooling rate to avoid kinetic over-densification.

REQUESTED >=2x slower melt->300 K cool is NOT expressible via decided_params or generate_equilibration_workflow: the cool ramp N_STEPS=steps_npt is atom-count-tiered (server.py ~L1154-1170, L1345-1358) and there is no cool_steps/npt_cool_steps argument; t_equil_ns is display-only in gen_prompt.py (L280), never threaded into the ramp. add_melt_npt=true provides a PARTIAL ~1.28x slowdown on the glass-formation descent (final npt_cool shortens from 620->300 K to 550->300 K over the same steps_npt; server.py L1330-1343 vs L1345-1358), CONDITIONAL on the orchestrator passing the --add_melt_npt CLI flag to gen_prompt (it is read from argv L253, NOT from decided_params via _pick — see equil stage orchestrator_note). If the flag is omitted, the melt branch does not fire and there is ZERO cooling mitigation (standard 620->300 K cool), making the equil-check density-detection gate the sole safety net. Full fix requires a cool-ramp step override (code change) or a manual intermediate cool stage — recorded as the dominant uncertainty, deferred to critic/orchestrator.

check_equilibration_comprehensive.overall_pass=True is a CONVERGENCE gate, not an ACCURACY gate — a run can converge tightly to 1.19 g/cm3 and pass (this is why the prior run failed silently). Therefore the equil-check stage adds an explicit density-target band (PE exp 0.95 g/cm3) so over-densification is autonomously DETECTED, per revision.md L20 ('test whether the agent autonomously detects over-densification ... or add it if it does not'). Density well above ~0.95 must route to /recover, not be silently extended past.

alternatives.choice: standard PHYC deterministic equilibration (t_equil_ns=5, npt_prod_ns=5, no melt hold)
alternatives.known_error: produced ~1.19 g/cm3 (+25% over experimental 0.95) in the prior PE run — rejected

planned_stages.orchestrator_note: REQUIRED: the equil-stage gen_prompt.py invocation MUST include the CLI flag --add_melt_npt. gen_prompt.py reads add_melt_npt from argv (L253), NOT from the plan's decided_params (no _pick/cls fallback; apply_plan does not thread it). Without --add_melt_npt the melt branch never fires: the 550 K melt-density hold (npt_melt) is dropped AND the ~1.28x cooling slowdown (the only implementable cooling mitigation) is lost, leaving the standard 620->300 K cool. decided_params.add_melt_npt=true records intent; the CLI flag activates it. melt_npt_ns=2.0 IS picked from the plan once the branch fires.

success_criteria.equil_verdict: PASS, OR EXTEND when blocked SOLELY by the melt-NVT C(t) dynamic-autocorrelation gate AND all static conformational/packing criteria below are met (high-DP rubbery-melt static-equilibration acceptance; applies to flexible high-DP classes PHYC/PDIE at DP >= ~80).
success_criteria.check_equilibration_comprehensive.overal: advisory_non_blocking — the tool's overall_pass embeds the melt-NVT C(t) full-decay sub-check, which is unreachable in feasible MD for a DP-120 (~3-6 entanglement) PE melt (KWW tau_relax ~88 ns extrapolated from PE1; full decorrelation would need ~360 ns NVT ~23 days — infeasible, and the standard 'extend 1-2 ns' remedy cannot close an 88 ns gap). overall_pass is therefore DEMOTED to advisory: it must NOT by itself FAIL the stage or trigger the extend loop when the static criteria below pass. ct_min_decay_melt=0.25 is retained in decided_params only as the tool's input; it does NOT re-impose a pass/fail gate here.

static_equilibration_criteria.rationale: decision_policy.json:policies.equilibration.evaluate requires 'chain_statistic_convergence' (a STATIC conformational-statistics test), NOT dynamic terminal decorrelation. The requested properties (density, Tg, K) are governed by equilibrium chain dimensions and packing, not by terminal chain reptation. This mirrors the validated PKTN/PEEK rigid-backbone screening-grade acceptance (static-structure + density gate, C(t)/MSD demoted to advisory). PE1 (replicate 1) confirmed this acceptance with static criteria passed; PE2 applies the same framework with fresh independent microstate.

characteristic_ratio_c_infinity.target: within ~10% of the TEMPERATURE-MATCHED, FINITE-N flexible-PE ideal C_n at the 550 K melt (~6.7; range ~6.7 @ 550 K to ~7.4 @ 300 K).
characteristic_ratio_c_infinity.reconciliation_note: polymer_rules.json/D-01 cite Ramos2015 asymptotic C_inf=8.2 @ 500 K and experimental 7.4-8.1; those are the INFINITE-CHAIN limit. A measured C_n for a DP-120 PE chain (n_bb=239 bonds) at 550 K is a FINITE-N value (C_n < C_inf) and legitimately sits below the asymptotic 8.2 and at the low edge of the experimental band. Gate against the temperature-matched finite-N ideal (~6.7), NOT the asymptotic 8.2 — a value near 6.7-7.4 is PASS. Source: 10.1021/acs.macromol.5b00823.

static_equilibration_criteria.advisory_metrics: C(t) decay, MSD diffusivity, MSID slope, Rg chain-chain CV, apparent C_inf — all advisory/non-blocking per PVC1-corrected gate set (2026-06-20)

success_criteria.dynamic_relaxation_caveat: tau_relax ~88 ns (KWW, from PE1 extrapolation) and MSD subdiffusion are RECORDED as a dynamic lower-bound caveat ONLY, non-blocking. Implication: terminal chain relaxation is incomplete, which can leave a residual frozen-in conformational memory; this does not affect equilibrium chain dimensions or packing, the quantities governing density/Tg/K. Flag the caveat in the run summary.
success_criteria.over_densification_detection: Density well above ~0.95 g/cm3 (e.g. >=1.05, repeating the prior +25% failure) must be flagged as over-densification and routed to /recover, NOT silently accepted. overall_pass=True alone is insufficient — it is a convergence gate, not an accuracy gate. This detection is the primary safety net because the requested 2x-slower cool is only ~1.28x implementable (see D-05). NOTE: this density gate (target 0.95, max_acceptable 1.05) is UNCHANGED by the static-equilibration revision.

findings: APPROVED (round 3, terminal). Gate verified independently: PHYC confidence=high => expected mode deterministic; plan declares reasoned. This is an UP-scrutiny override (more review, not skipped), NOT a confidence-gate bypass — the gate's anti-pattern is deterministic-on-non-high (under-scrutiny). Reasoned is REQUIRED here: the revision.md Priority-0 over-densification remediation (1.19 vs exp 0.95 g/cm3) is not expressible by deterministic transcription. PE1 user-approved precedent. No escalate on this.

Round count: the 2-round cap governs the revise LOOP (planner<->critic churn), not approval. At round 3 the effective choice is approved-or-escalate; escalate requires a NEW hard require-violation with no in-pipeline fix. None exists — the round-1 D-05 rubbery overall_pass demotion is cured by policies.equilibration.require_rubbery (authorized 2026-06-22, verified present in decision_policy.json). PE is rubbery at the gate (exp Tg~195K < T_workflow_K=300K); the plan's equil-check static criteria (density block-SEM/homogeneity-CV/energy gating; C(t)/MSD/tau_relax/MSID/Rg-CV advisory) CONFORM to require_rubbery.

Per-decision sweep clean: D-01_ff (4/4, Ramos2015 DOI), D-02_charges (3/3 incl charge_method_cost), D-03_electrostatics (3/3, source_doi 10.1021/jp972543+, lj_cut justified for |q|=0 C/H), D-04_system_size (DP=120>Fox-Flory, nchain=20>10), D-05_convergence (require_rubbery-conformant), D-06_tg (4/4, sweep 100-450K brackets exp Tg~195K), D-07_property_method (4/4, Murnaghan-rubbery, Wu2019 DOI, alternatives present). Stage schema valid: all 8 stages have stage/track/success_criteria; tracks match track_map. properties match planned_stages. Dominant uncertainty (kinetic_over_densification) named; fast_density_screen is a valid probes key. Hardware unpinned => TraPPE policy default (engine=gpu/mpi=1/1 GPU); ~4800 UA atoms <10k; values_are_benchmarked=false but no non-default pin => no probe required.

NON-BLOCKING: D-01_ff and D-03_electrostatics carry alternatives:[] (both evidence_required:true). Self-justifying for an apolar TraPPE-UA |q|=0 C/H backbone (pppm/all-atom alternatives physically pointless); present in the approved PE1 parent. At round 3 no revise path exists; a cosmetic gap cannot justify killing a legitimate replicate. Noted, not blocking.

NON-BLOCKING (orchestrator-scoped): equil cooling mitigation rests on the --add_melt_npt CLI flag firing (argv, not decided_params) AND the equil-check over-densification gate (target 0.95, max_acceptable 1.05 => /recover). Both documented in the plan's equil orchestrator_note and equil-check success_criteria. The critic cannot enforce the orchestrator's invocation; the note's presence is sufficient.

clone_notes: seeds updated to replicate-2 triple (emc_cell=1004, SEED_HOT=1005, SEED_COLD=1006)

equil-check static_equilibration_criteria: stripped PE1-specific observed values (6.94 C_inf, 0.194 Rg-CV, 0.128 homogeneity CV, 1.18 MSID, 0.041 P2, 0.860 density); kept targets/gates/thresholds and reconciliation logic intact
advisory_metrics field added to make the PVC1-corrected gate/advisory split explicit (packing=gating; diffusion-limited=advisory)
critique reset to proposed/rounds=0 for fresh critic review
no hardware override added: PHYC=trappe, ~4800 UA atoms (<10k), by_forcefield default (engine=gpu/mpi=1/1 GPU) correct; values_are_benchmarked=false so no benchmark probe required
